sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **Help Scout** connector integrates Shielva with the [Help Scout API v2](https://developer.helpscout.com/mailbox-api/) to sync conversations, customers, mailboxes, users, and tags into your Shielva knowledge base.

Help Scout is a customer support platform used by teams to manage shared inboxes, live chat, and customer relationships. This connector exposes **7 API methods** across 4 resource domains — Conversations, Customers, Mailboxes, and Users — and normalizes all data to Shielva's `ConnectorDocument` schema for downstream search and AI workflows.

**Key capabilities:**
- Full conversation sync with subject, preview, customer info, assignee, mailbox, and tags
- Customer sync with contact details (email, phone from embedded _embedded arrays), company, and background notes
- Mailbox listing for inbox management
- User/agent listing for team management
- HAL+JSON pagination (`_links.next.href`) for all paginated resources
- OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow — no user redirect required, fully server-side
- Auto-refresh bearer token when within 30 seconds of expiry
- Typed exception hierarchy with automatic retry (exponential backoff, 3 attempts)
- Auth errors never retried — fail-fast on invalid credentials

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
Get the Help Scout connector running in under 5 minutes.

### 1. Create a Help Scout OAuth App

1. Log in to Help Scout → click your name → **Your Profile** → **My Apps**.
2. Click **Create My App**, fill in a name and any redirect URL.
3. Copy the **App ID** and **App Secret**.

### 2. Install

In the Shielva integration builder:
1. Navigate to **Integrations → Help Scout**.
2. Enter your **App ID** and **App Secret**.
3. Click **Install**.

The connector calls `GET /users/me` to validate credentials and returns the connected user's name on success.

### 3. Run a sync

```python
from connector import HelpScoutConnector

async with HelpScoutConnector(
    config={"client_id": "YOUR_APP_ID", "client_secret": "YOUR_APP_SECRET"}
) as conn:
    result = await conn.sync(full=True, kb_id="kb_support_001")
    print(f"Synced {result.documents_synced} documents")
```

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
The Help Scout connector uses **OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials** flow.

### Credential fields

| Field | Key | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| App ID | `client_id` | string | Yes | From Help Scout → Your Profile → My Apps |
| App Secret | `client_secret` | password | Yes | From Help Scout → Your Profile → My Apps |

### Token flow

```
POST https://api.helpscout.net/v2/oauth2/token
grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=...&client_secret=...
→ { "access_token": "...", "expires_in": 7200 }
```

The `HelpScoutHTTPClient` automatically:
1. Exchanges credentials for a bearer token on first use.
2. Injects `Authorization: Bearer {token}` on every request.
3. Refreshes the token when within 30 seconds of expiry.

### No scopes needed

Help Scout's Client Credentials grant automatically provides access to all resources the app owner can access. No scope configuration is required.

### Security

- App credentials are stored encrypted in the Shielva vault and never logged.
- Bearer tokens are held in memory only and never persisted to disk.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
The Help Scout connector requires two install fields.

### Install fields

```json
{
  "client_id": "your_help_scout_app_id",
  "client_secret": "your_help_scout_app_secret"
}
```

### Internal defaults

| Parameter | Default | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `TIMEOUT` | `30s` | HTTP request timeout |
| `MAX_RETRIES` | `3` | Retry attempts on transient failures |
| `RETRY_BACKOFF` | Exponential | 1s → 2s → 4s with ±0.5s jitter |
| `TOKEN_REFRESH_BUFFER` | `30s` | Refresh token this many seconds before expiry |
| `TOKEN_URL` | `https://api.helpscout.net/v2/oauth2/token` | OAuth token endpoint |
| `API_BASE` | `https://api.helpscout.net/v2` | API base URL |

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: The connector exposes 7 methods across 4 resource domains.

children.title: list_conversations(**kwargs)
children.content:
Returns all conversations across all pages.

```python
conversations = await conn.list_conversations(status="active")
```

Paginates automatically via HAL `_links.next.href` until no next page.

children.title: get_conversation(conversation_id)
children.content:
Returns a single conversation by numeric ID.

```python
conv = await conn.get_conversation("1001")
```

children.title: list_customers(**kwargs)
children.content:
Returns all customers across all pages.

```python
customers = await conn.list_customers()
```

children.title: list_mailboxes()
children.content:
Returns all mailboxes the account can access.

```python
mailboxes = await conn.list_mailboxes()
```

children.title: list_users()
children.content:
Returns all users/agents across all pages.

```python
users = await conn.list_users()
```

children.title: install()
children.content:
Validates credentials and returns an `InstallResult`. Internally calls `authenticate()` then `GET /users/me`.

```python
result = await conn.install()
print(result.health, result.message)
```

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
Pings `GET /users/me` and returns a `HealthCheckResult`.

```python
health = await conn.health_check()
assert health.health == ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY
```

sections.title: Pagination
Sync Engine
sections.content:
The `sync()` method sequentially syncs all resource types in this order:

1. **Conversations** — paginated; failure aborts and returns `FAILED`
2. **Customers** — paginated; failure returns `PARTIAL`
3. **Mailboxes** — single request; failure is non-fatal (supplemental data)
4. **Users** — paginated; failure is non-fatal (supplemental data)

Each item is normalized to a `ConnectorDocument` and optionally pushed to the knowledge base via `_ingest_document(doc, kb_id)` when `kb_id` is provided.

```python
result = await conn.sync(full=True, kb_id="kb_support_001")
print(result.status, result.documents_synced, result.documents_failed)
```

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
### Exception hierarchy

```
HelpScoutError (base)
├── HelpScoutAuthError        — 401/403 or OAuth token failure
├── HelpScoutNetworkError     — 5xx, connection errors, timeouts
├── HelpScoutNotFoundError    — 404
└── HelpScoutRateLimitError   — 429; carries retry_after
```

### Retry behavior

- Auth errors: **never retried** (require human action — re-check credentials)
- Rate limit: retried using `Retry-After` header value if present, else exponential backoff
- Network errors: retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff + jitter

### Status mapping

| HTTP status | Exception |
|-------------|----------|
| 401 / 403 | `HelpScoutAuthError` |
| 404 | `HelpScoutNotFoundError` |
| 429 | `HelpScoutRateLimitError` |
| 5xx | `HelpScoutNetworkError` |
| Other | `HelpScoutError` |

sections.title: Document Schema
sections.content:
All resources are normalized to `ConnectorDocument`:

```python
@dataclass
class ConnectorDocument:
    source_id: str          # sha256("<type>:<id>")[:16]
    title: str              # Human-readable title
    content: str            # Searchable text content
    connector_id: str       # Stamped by connector at sync time
    tenant_id: str          # Stamped by connector at sync time
    source_url: str         # Deep link into Help Scout
    metadata: dict          # Resource-specific fields
```

### Source ID stability

Source IDs are deterministic SHA-256 prefixes:

| Resource | Formula |
|----------|--------|
| Conversation | `sha256("conversation:{id}")[:16]` |
| Customer | `sha256("customer:{id}")[:16]` |
| Mailbox | `sha256("mailbox:{id}")[:16]` |
| User | `sha256("user:{id}")[:16]` |
